description_long: This all-terrain vehicle features a robust, boxy chassis with six large, rugged wheels designed for maximum traction on uneven surfaces. The body is predominantly white with metallic and black accents, and it is equipped with a variety of articulated arms and sensor modules, suggesting advanced navigation and manipulation capabilities. The wheels are mounted on an independent suspension system, allowing for significant ground clearance and adaptability to rough terrain. The vehicle's exterior is adorned with various scientific instruments, cameras, and what appears to be a mast with additional sensors. The rear section includes a set of cylindrical components and radiators, likely for power and thermal management. Overall, the design is highly functional, with exposed mechanical elements and a utilitarian aesthetic suited for exploration and technical tasks.
description: A six-wheeled, white and metallic all-terrain vehicle with articulated arms, advanced sensors, and rugged tires, built for exploration and technical operations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vehicle's central mast, forward-facing sensors, and the symmetrical arrangement of the six wheels.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the articulated arm, side-mounted instruments, and the full length of the chassis with visible suspension.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel, cylindrical components, and the rear wheel configuration.
description_view_3: Right side view featuring the extended arm, additional sensors, and the detailed structure of the rear section.
attribute_tire_tread_pattern: The tires have a pronounced, blocky all-terrain tread pattern with deep grooves and ridges, optimized for traction on loose or rocky surfaces.
